International Flavors & Fragrances (IFF) is a global powerhouse dedicated to delivering sustainable innovations that elevate everyday products. Their mission in the “Taste” division is to craft unique, technology-enabled flavor designs that define food and beverage brands, shape market trends, and fuel commercial success. IFF offers a broad range of natural solutions, accelerating growth in the food and beverage sectors globally. Building your IFF Career starts in a modern, supportive learning environment surrounded by experienced application teams. IFF values the diverse mosaic of its workforce. They strive for an inclusive workplace where every colleague is encouraged to bring their authentic self to work, maximizing the creative potential of the team.
[...]
### Food Science Internships – Key Responsibilities
[...]
Unlike many Food Science Internships that are purely observational, this role is highly interactive. You will be placed into one of three specific application labs: Beverage, Dairy, or Culinary.
[...]
- Prototype Production: Assist application specialists in the preparation and production of physical prototypes, ranging from beverages, yoghurts, and ice cream to vegan foods, soups, sauces, and snacks.
- Sensory Analysis: Actively analyze, taste, and accurately describe laboratory prototypes and relevant market products.
[...]
- Data Management: Carry out precise record-keeping of laboratory formulations and application work using IFF’s dedicated software systems.
[...]
- Laboratory Upkeep: Support essential general lab activities, including safe stock management, handling supplies, and maintaining strict hygiene and housekeeping standards.
[...]
- Project Ownership: Take part in a specific, tailored assignment or prototype development project for a real customer, aligned with your availability, studies, and personal interests.
[...]
To secure this competitive internship for Food Technology students, you need an ongoing academic foundation paired with a genuine curiosity for food innovation.
[...]
- Education: Currently pursuing an ongoing food-related or science-based degree (or an equivalent qualification).
- Technical Acumen: Strong mathematical and computer skills, with the ability to perform laboratory work accurately and safely.
- Work Ethic: A self-motivated, proactive approach to work. You must be able to work independently, manage tasks responsibly, and maintain a strong commitment to quality and detail.
- Passion: A deep, demonstrated interest in food, raw ingredients, and complex flavors.
[...]
In summary, this Flavor Application role is a premier internship for Food Technology students looking to bridge the gap between academic theory and commercial food production. By securing this position, you will gain exposure to real customer projects in modern application laboratories, setting a strong foundation for a long-term IFF Career. Do not miss the chance to stand out in the competitive landscape of European Food Science Internships—apply before the May 31st deadline to begin crafting the flavors of tomorrow.
